summary: Iteration 20: Conway API Key Input Feature - All backend tests pass (11/11). Frontend Genesis screen verified: Conway API Key panel shows 'connected and valid' state with key prefix, MongoDB persistence mentioned, all 6 provisioning steps visible, Buy Credits link opens external site correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: POST /api/credits/set-key correctly validates key format (must start with cnwy_)
POST /api/credits/set-key validates against Conway API before persisting
MongoDB persistence for Conway API key implemented in platform_config collection
Server startup _restore_conway_key_from_db() correctly restores key from MongoDB
Frontend Conway API Key panel shows green confirmation when key is valid
Frontend shows 'survives redeployments' message confirming MongoDB persistence
All 6 provisioning steps have data-testid attributes for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 20 verified the Conway API key input feature. Key is stored in MongoDB (platform_config collection) and auto-restored on startup. Genesis screen shows 'connected and valid' green state when key is configured. All endpoints tested: set-key, key-status, balance, verify, history. Note: Key has 0 credits which is expected for this test environment.
backend_apis_tested: POST /api/credits/set-key (invalid format) - 200 OK (success=false, error mentions cnwy_)

new_features_tested.api_key_input_field: Genesis screen shows key input when not configured, green confirmation when valid
new_features_tested.mongodb_persistence: Key stored in platform_config collection, auto-restored on server startup
new_features_tested.key_validation: Keys validated against Conway API before persisting
new_features_tested.key_format_check: Invalid format (not starting with cnwy_) rejected with error message
